summary: Updated the package publishing GitHub Actions workflow path filters so push and pull_request triggers share one YAML anchor and include additional Cargo and Rust-tool paths.

key_files.role: Only changed file; GitHub Actions workflow whose push and pull_request path filters were deduplicated and expanded.

what_changed.text: Replaced separate push and pull_request path lists with a shared YAML anchor named common_paths and an alias for pull_request.
Expanded package rebuild trigger paths from Cargo.lock at repository root to **/Cargo.lock and added **/Cargo.toml.
Added Rust tool source files under tools/**/*.rs to the workflow path filters.
Quoted the workflow path patterns in the new shared list.

smart_moves.text: Used a YAML anchor and alias to keep push and pull_request package rebuild path filters in sync.
Broadened Cargo-related rebuild triggers to nested lockfiles and manifests, which fits the PR title's stated goal of expanding package rebuild condition paths.

naming_conventions.text: For shared GitHub Actions path filters in this workflow, the PR used the YAML anchor name common_paths.

follow_up_queries: GitHub Actions package rebuild paths Cargo.toml Cargo.lock
.github/workflows path filters pull_request push YAML anchor

This is a CI/tooling workflow change, not a decompilation source change; decomp lessons and assembly/matching tactics are not applicable.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting shared path filters for package publishing workflow
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2680 shows .github/workflows/publish-packages.yml using a YAML anchor named common_paths so push and pull_request triggers share the same package rebuild path filter list, including .github workflow/package files, nested Cargo.lock, nested Cargo.toml, reqs/**/*.txt, tools/**/*.py, and tools/**/*.rs.
source_update_candidates.reason: This may be useful as a tooling-maintenance note if the curator maintains CI workflow conventions, but the PR evidence does not establish it as a global decomp standard.
